title: Oil Gains As Iran Prepares For Confrontation With Us
section_path: Oil Gains As Iran Prepares For Confrontation With Us
section_path_text: Oil Gains As Iran Prepares For Confrontation With Us
summary:
Main
A broad risk-on sentiment has returned to commodity markets, with prices rising across the sectors. Strong US economic data have supported not only growth commodities but also precious metals. By Daniel Hynes

Market Commentary
Crude oil prices rose on investor concerns over the final agreement between the US and Iran, which hinges on a potential nuclear deal.

children.title: Main
children.summary: A broad risk-on sentiment has returned to commodity markets, with prices rising across the sectors. Strong US economic data have supported not only growth commodities but also precious metals.

children.title: Market Commentary
children.summary: Crude oil prices rose on investor concerns over the final agreement between the US and Iran, which hinges on a potential nuclear deal. Iran is expected to present a comprehensive proposal within the next two weeks, while a report suggested that American military intervention could come sooner than expected.

children.title: Chart of the Day
children.summary: Russia's crude shipments have edged higher over the past couple of weeks, as a step-up in Ukrainian drone strikes on the country's refineries and a halt to piped deliveries to Hungary and Slovakia likely boosting export volumes. Exports averaged 3.39mb/d in the four weeks to 15 Feb, rising for a fourth week, but were still down from the pre-Christmas peak.
